# dFactory-Lab â€” Master Checklist

> The single source of truth for building **dFactory-Lab**: a server + UI that wraps the
> [dFactory](https://github.com/inclusionAI/dFactory) diffusion-LLM (dLLM) fine-tuning engine so a user can
> **download â†’ MoE-merge â†’ prepare data â†’ train (block-diffusion SFT) â†’ split â†’ export â†’ chat** without touching the CLI.
> The fine-tuning/merge/diffusion logic comes from **dFactory (Apache-2.0)**; the **Unsloth Studio** architecture
> (FastAPI + Tauri/React) is the *management UI reference, studied but not copied* (it is AGPL-3.0).

**Status: Phase 0 â€” Foundation.** Every phase ends with a git commit. Items map 1:1 to PRs (`Closes Â§T-12`).

## How to use this checklist

- **Check boxes** â€” GitHub renders `- [ ]`; check an item when its acceptance criteria are met and verified.
- **Item codes** are stable per section (`MH-1`, `T-12`â€¦); cite them in commits and PRs.
- **`P0`** blocks the MVP Â· **`P1`** core Â· **`P2`** polish.
- **`phN`** is the suggested build phase (table below). Layers: `backend` Â· `frontend` Â· `fullstack` Â· `infra` Â· `design`.
- Items are grounded in the real upstream source â€” file names, config keys (`block_diffusion_mode`, `noise_range_low/high`,
  `block_size`, `moe_implementation: fused`, `data_parallel_mode: fsdp2`), and exact script behavior
  (`scripts/moe_convertor.py`, `scripts/download_hf_model.py`, `scripts/build_gsm8k_dataset.py`, `train.sh`).

## Phase map

| ph | Phase | Repo state at end of phase |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | Foundation & Checklist | engine vendored, Checklist.md, project meta â€” **(this commit)** |
| 1 | Repo + backend skeleton + dev server | FastAPI app starts like Unsloth (run.py + banner), SQLite stores, job framework |
| 2 | Model hub + MoE merge/split | download, merge, split, inventory |
| 3 | Datasets & prep | ingest, conversational conversion, validation, preview |
| 4 | Training engine (dLLM SFT) | YAML builder, torchrun launcher, live metrics, resume |
| 5 | Export/merge/convert + packaging | automated split + modeling-file copy, packaging |
| 6 | Inference & chat | dLLM serving, masking/step params, history, compare |
| 7 | Frontend shell + design system | routing, layout, **locked distinct design system** |
| 8 | Frontend features | training/datasets/models/export/chat/playground screens |
| 9 | Auth/security/observability/distribution | auth, subprocess safety, audit, Tauri/web packaging |
| 10 | Testing/QA/docs/release | test suites, GPU smoke CI, docs site, v1 release |

## Design direction â€” pick one in Phase 7 (TBD)

The UI **must be visually distinct** from Unsloth/dFactory and **must reject the generic "AI-slop" black + blue
gradient SaaS look.** Three candidate directions; one is locked before frontend work begins (see Â§DS):

- **(A) Editorial Paper** â€” warm off-white paper base, ink-near-black text, a single warm accent (terracotta/saffron),
  serif display + grotesque body + mono. Light-first, magazine-grade.
- **(B) Signal Lab** â€” warm near-black canvas, phosphor amber/green foreground, mono-dominant, oscilloscope/CRT feel.
  Dark, but amber/green (not blue).
- **(C) Industrial Console** â€” neutral light-gray base, high-contrast data density, a single hazard accent
  (safety-orange), mono readouts, instrument-panel layout.

## 2. Backend Core & Server Lifecycle
<sub>26 items Â· section code `B`</sub>
**Background Jobs**
- [ ] **B-1** â€” Implement a job registry mapping job kinds to worker entrypoints  `P0` `ph2` `backend`
    - Build a JobRegistry (dict of kind -> (worker_module, queue_class)) covering the dFactory pipeline steps: download (hub/workers/hf_download.py wrapping scripts/download_hf_model.py), merge/split (scripts/moe_convertor.py), build-dataset (scripts/build_gsm8k_dataset.py), train (tasks/train_llada2_bd.py via torchrun), convert (moe_convertor split), copy-modeling-file. Each job kind registers spawn args, the config dict schema, and the expected event message types.
- [ ] **B-2** â€” Spawn each job in an isolated subprocess via mp.get_context('spawn')  `P0` `ph2` `backend`
    - Mirror core/training/worker.py and core/export/worker.py: each job runs in a fresh spawn subprocess (clean interpreter, no stale transformers/VeOmni module state), communicating via a cmd_queue + event_queue (or resp_queue). The orchestrator owns the Process handle for force_terminate(). Workers must set TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false, PYTHONWARNINGS=ignore, PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 before ML imports.
- [ ] **B-3** â€” Pipe worker stdout/stderr to the parent via fd-level capture  `P1` `ph2` `backend`
    - Port core/export/worker.py._setup_log_capture(resp_queue): dup fds 1/2, os.pipe() a reader pair, dup2 the write ends so the worker and any torchrun/VeOmni child inherit redirected fds, spawn daemon _reader threads that split on \n/\r (so tqdm bars update) and push {type:'log', stream, line, ts} to the queue. Echo to the saved fds so the server session log keeps full output. Gate forwarding with a threading.Event so bootstrap import noise is suppressed.
**Entry & Launcher**
- [ ] **B-4** â€” Implement backend/run.py entry that launches uvicorn the Unsloth way  `P0` `ph1` `backend`
    - Create backend/run.py mirroring unsloth/studio/backend/run.py's run_server(): construct a uvicorn.Config(app, host, port, server_header=False, access_log=False), wrap a _ReadyServer(uvicorn.Server) subclass that sets ready_event on startup, run loop.run_until_complete(_server.serve()) in a daemon Thread, and block on ready_event until sockets bind. Accept --host/--port/--api-only/--silent args via argparse (default 127.0.0.1:8900). Must be runnable as `python backend/run.py` and `uvicorn main:app`.
- [ ] **B-5** â€” Auto-find a free port and warn on conflict  `P1` `ph1` `backend`
    - Port Unsloth's _is_port_free(host, port) (bind probe + loopback 127.0.0.1/::1 check for wildcard binds) and _find_free_port(host, start, max_attempts=20); when the requested port is busy, print a banner box naming the blocking PID (via psutil net_connections when available) and switch to the next free port. _PID_FILE at $DFACTORY_HOME/dfactory.pid written on bind and removed at exit.
**FastAPI App**
- [ ] **B-6** â€” Implement FastAPI app factory in main.py with lifespan context  `P0` `ph1` `backend`
    - Create main.py exporting `app = FastAPI(title='dFactory-Lab Backend', lifespan=lifespan)` plus a `setup_frontend(app, build_path)` helper. lifespan() must, in order: clear any compiled cache, detect hardware/GPU, run cleanup_orphaned_jobs() to mark crashed jobs as errored, init the API-token/RSA key material, yield, then on teardown call run_lifespan_shutdown() to cancel downloads and tear down training/export subprocesses. Include a `if __name__ == '__main__': run_server()` convenience.
- [ ] **B-7** â€” Add request LoggingMiddleware and SecurityHeadersMiddleware  `P1` `ph1` `backend`
    - Port loggers/handlers.LoggingMiddleware (pure-ASGI, dedups repeated 2xx GETs within UNSLOTH_STUDIO_ACCESS_LOG_DEDUP_MS, logs 'request_completed' with method/path/status/process_time_ms) and main.SecurityHeadersMiddleware (sets Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy no-referrer, server='dfactory-lab'). Both must be pure ASGI (not BaseHTTPMiddleware) so SSE/WebSocket streams are not buffered.
- [ ] **B-8** â€” Add MaxBodyMiddleware capping request bodies on protected prefixes  `P1` `ph1` `backend`
    - Port main.MaxBodyMiddleware (raw ASGI) so POST/PUT/PATCH under /api/models/merge, /api/train, /api/export, /api/datasets/upload, /api/settings are capped by default_request_body_limit_bytes(), with the upload passthrough prefixes returning 411 (no Content-Length) / 413 (oversized) and a larger upload_request_limit_bytes(). Protects against oversized checkpoint uploads without breaking multipart.
**Graceful Shutdown**
- [ ] **B-9** â€” Implement POST /api/shutdown and a trigger_shutdown callable  `P1` `ph1` `backend`
    - Port main.shutdown_server: auth-gated endpoint that creates asyncio.create_task(_delayed_shutdown()) which sleeps 0.2s (lets the response flush) then calls app.state.trigger_shutdown(); set that callable in run_server before binding sockets so the endpoint is ready at first readiness. When not launched via run_server (direct uvicorn), fall back to os.kill(os.getpid(), SIGTERM). Returns {status:'shutting_down'}.
- [ ] **B-10** â€” Implement _graceful_shutdown() tearing down all subprocess workers  `P0` `ph1` `backend`
    - Port run.py._graceful_shutdown(server): in order set server.should_exit=True, then _shutdown_subprocess(timeout=5.0) on the inference/export orchestrators, force_terminate() the training backend (the torchrun child), kill any chat/llama server, stop tunnels, and finally a backstop terminate_all() sweep of adopted children. Register SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGBREAK handlers that restore SIG_DFL then call _graceful_shutdown + _shutdown_event.set() so a second signal force-quits if teardown stalls.
- [ ] **B-11** â€” Add resilient run_lifespan_shutdown() that never raises  `P1` `ph1` `backend`
    - Port utils/lifespan_shutdown.run_lifespan_shutdown(terminate_downloads, clear_compiled_cache, hw_module): copy context, run terminate_downloads via loop.run_in_executor guarded so a dead-executor RuntimeError runs it inline and any body exception is logged not re-raised, then clear hw_module.DEVICE and clear_compiled_cache each in its own try. Verify an exception in one step still runs the next.

## 3. Reliability, Orchestration & Unattended Operation
<sub>19 items Â· section code `R`</sub>
**CI / smoke**
- [ ] **R-1** â€” Add a GPU smoke CI job running 2 training steps on a toy merged MoE checkpoint  `P1` `ph10` `infra`
    - Backend CI is CPU-only (pytest excluding GPU tests), so the real torchrun+VeOmni+FSDP2+block-diffusion path is never exercised in CI. Add an optional self-hosted GPU workflow (matrix mini config, num_experts=4 toy checkpoint, num_train_epochs with save_steps to trigger one Checkpointer.save, then one split) asserting: a checkpoint dir appears, hf_ckpt is loadable, and split reproduces expert shapes. Acceptance: the smoke job catches a VeOmni upgrade or a block_diffusion_attn_mask regression that CPU CI cannot.
**Checkpoint cadence / crash recovery**
- [ ] **R-2** â€” Bound worst-case work loss by enforcing a save_steps cadence tied to wall-time  `P1` `ph4` `backend`
    - Checkpoints write only at global_step % save_steps==0 or per save_epochs (train_llada2_bd.py L513-530, L535-549); with log_steps=1 and the default save_epochs=1, a crash mid-epoch discards the entire epoch. The config builder must warn when save_steps is unset on runs >1 epoch, and offer a 'max minutes between checkpoints' advisor that computes save_steps = ceil(target_minutes * steps_per_minute). Acceptance: a 2-hour flash run offered save_steps so a crash at 1h55m loses <=~5 min, not 2h.
- [ ] **R-3** â€” Implement checkpoint pruning to cap disk under output_dir/checkpoints/global_step_*  `P1` `ph4` `backend`
    - Checkpointer.save writes a fresh global_step_XXX dir (full model+optimizer+extra_state, ckpt_manager=dcp) at every save_steps and every save_epochs boundary; for flash (100B) each dcp shard is tens of GB and the dir grows unbounded. Add a keep_last N policy (configurable, default 3) that deletes older global_step_* dirs except the highest step, guarded to never delete the latest hf_ckpt-bearing dir. Acceptance: a long flash run with save_steps set never exceeds N*shard_size on disk.
**Crash recovery**
- [ ] **R-4** â€” Run cleanup_orphaned_jobs across ALL job kinds (download/merge/dataset/train/split), not just training  `P1` `ph2` `backend`
    - The reconciliation item covers UPDATE training_runs SET status='error' WHERE status='running' on startup, but the job registry also has download, merge, split, and build-dataset jobs that crash mid-way (e.g. a partial merged-expert shard, a half-written JSONL). Extend startup reconciliation to scan the jobs table for any status in {running,queued} and mark them errored with 'Server restarted during <kind>', and delete partially-written safetensors/JSONL artifacts whose mtime postdates the job start. Acceptance: after a crash, no job row is left 'running' and no half-written shard is served as complete.
- [ ] **R-5** â€” Verify torch_rng_state restore path handles CPU-only RNG (missing CUDA rng state) on resume  `P2` `ph4` `backend`
    - Resume restores torch.set_rng_state(extra_state['torch_rng_state']) (train_llada2_bd.py L357) but only the CPU RNG; the data_transform noise sampling (sft_noise_transition sigma per example) and block_diffusion masking use RNG, and CUDA RNG state is not checkpointed. Document and, where determinism matters, extend extra_state with torch.cuda.get_rng_state_all(). Acceptance: a resumed run's masking schedule is reproducible when enable_full_determinism=true; without it, document that noise differs post-resume (acceptable for SFT but not for deterministic CI).
**Dataset determinism**
- [ ] **R-6** â€” Pin and persist the dataset build (build_gsm8k_dataset) so re-runs are byte-identical  `P1` `ph3` `backend`
    - build_gsm8k_dataset.py calls load_dataset('openai/gsm8k','main') with no revision pin; HF dataset snapshots drift, changing row order/content and silently altering train_size/train_steps (which compute_train_steps derives). The build endpoint must pin a datasets revision, record the gsm8k commit SHA into the JSONL header/provenance, and the trainer's build_iterative_dataset(seed=args.train.seed) then operates on a frozen artifact. Acceptance: rebuilding the dataset 6 months apart yields identical files; a stale dataset is detected by a SHA mismatch on the training run.
**Distribution / packaging**
- [ ] **R-7** â€” Pin the VeOmni submodule to a release tag and gate engine compatibility on torch<2.11  `P1` `ph1` `infra`
    - VeOmni is added as a submodule at HEAD (no pinned tag in the existing submodule item), and train_llada2_bd.py imports veomni.optim, veomni.models, veomni.utils.arguments directly â€” an upstream API change breaks training silently. Pin VeOmni to a specific commit/tag in .gitmodules, add an engine-compatibility check (import veomni; assert veomni.__version__ >= X) at startup, and gate the bundled torch pin (<2.11 from the packaging item) against VeOmni's declared torch range. Acceptance: a `uv sync` + `git submodule update` reproduces a known-good VeOmni, and an incompatible VeOmni fails startup with a clear message.
**Download robustness**
- [ ] **R-8** â€” Make HF download resumable and idempotent via snapshot_download resume_transfer  `P0` `ph2` `backend`
    - download_hf_model.py calls snapshot_download(repo_id, local_dir=...) with no allow_patterns/resume/max_workers; a 100B flash download (hundreds of GB) interrupted mid-shard restarts from scratch by default. Wrap it with resume_download=True (huggingface_hub >=0.24), pass allow_patterns=['*.safetensors','*.json','tokenizer*','*.txt'] to skip non-weight blobs, and surface per-file resume state. Acceptance: killing the download process at 60% and relaunching resumes from the last partial shard, not byte 0.
- [ ] **R-9** â€” Pre-flight HF token / gated-repo access before starting a multi-hour download  `P1` `ph2` `backend`
    - snapshot_download raises on a gated repo without a token only after connecting; for LLaDA2-preview (potentially gated) this surfaces as an opaque 401 minutes into setup. The download service must call HfApi().model_info(repo_id, token=) first and return 402/403 with 'Repository {repo_id} requires a gated-access token; set HF_TOKEN' before launching snapshot_download. Acceptance: a gated repo fails the pre-flight in <2s, not after a partial download.
**Job concurrency**
- [ ] **R-10** â€” Serialize GPU-holding jobs (train + inference + merge/split) via a GPU-resource semaphore, not just single-training-job gating  `P1` `ph6` `backend`
    - Existing gating is 'one training job at a time' plus the train-vs-chat coexistence probe, but merge/split (moe_convertor loads the full state_dict to CPU, sets torch.set_default_dtype(bfloat16)) and inference (holds model on GPU) can overlap a training launch. Add a GPU-occupancy registry: train acquires all selected GPUs exclusively; merge/split acquire CPU+no-GPU but block if RAM < input_size; inference acquires its GPU set. The start endpoints check the registry and return 409 'GPU(s) X held by job Y' instead of launching a contending torchrun. Acceptance: starting merge while training runs returns 409 naming the holding job.
**Multi-node**
- [ ] **R-11** â€” Provide a node-join health check before torchrun rendezvous completes  `P2` `ph4` `backend`
    - Multi-node launch (--rdzv_endpoint) blocks indefinitely if a node can't reach MASTER_ADDR:MASTER_PORT or has a mismatched NPROC_PER_NODE, surfacing only as a hang. Add a pre-launch reachability probe (TCP connect from each node to MASTER_ADDR:MASTER_PORT with a 5s timeout) and a NPROC_PER_NODE equality check across nodes via the shared config; fail fast with 'Node <rank> unreachable' or 'NPROC mismatch: node0=8 node1=4' before torchrun is invoked. Acceptance: a misconfigured second node fails the probe in <10s instead of hanging rdzv.
**NCCL / distributed robustness**
- [ ] **R-12** â€” Inject NCCL_ASYNC_ERROR_HANDLING and TORCH_NCCL_BLOCKING_WAIT env into the torchrun subprocess  `P0` `ph4` `backend`
    - train.sh only exports TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false and TORCH_NCCL_AVOID_RECORD_STREAMS=1; a hung rank (typical in MoE expert routing on uneven EP shards) will stall torchrun indefinitely with no traceback. The launcher must also set NCCL_ASYNC_ERROR_HANDLING=1 (fast abort on collective error) and TORCH_NCCL_BLOCKING_WAIT=1, and rely on train.sh's rdzv scheme. Acceptance: a SIGKILL of one rank aborts the job within seconds with a logged NCCL error rather than hanging until the cloud host reclaims.
- [ ] **R-13** â€” Add an orchestrator-level wall-clock timeout that aborts a stalled torchrun  `P0` `ph4` `backend`
    - train_llada2_bd.py has no max_time cap (it loops num_train_epochs fixed steps), and train.sh has no watchdog. Port the _PROGRESS_STALL_TIMEOUT_POLLS=1800 (30 min at 1 poll/sec) stall detector from routes/training.py into the dFactory launcher: track last global_step seen in log.txt, and when a run is stepping but no new step appears for the stall window, SIGTERM the process group and mark the run failed with reason 'progress_stall'. Acceptance: a simulated NCCL hang marks the run errored in <=31 min, not at host-reclaim time.
**Observability**
- [ ] **R-14** â€” Surface torchrun per-rank exit codes and the rank that failed in the run failure record  `P1` `ph4` `backend`
    - On non-zero torchrun exit, the crash-detection item marks the run failed with a log tail, but torchrun reports which rank(s) died (e.g. 'Rank 2 failed with exit code 1') and the orchestrator discards that detail. Parse torchrun's final summary line to record failed_rank, exit_code, and the failing rank's last log lines (from the fd-captured stderr, tagged by rank) into the run's error_message. Acceptance: a multi-GPU failure record names 'rank 2 / exit code -6 (SIGABRT)' so the operator knows which GPU/node to inspect, not just a generic 'torchrun exited 1'.
- [ ] **R-15** â€” Forward torchrun/VeOmni worker logs with rank tagging to the SSE stream  `P2` `ph4` `backend`
    - The fd-capture item pipes stdout/stderr but torchrun prefixes rank logs inconsistently (logger.info_rank0 vs logger.info on all ranks). Configure VeOmni/torchrun so each worker line is tagged [rank=N] and the SSE event payload includes rank, so the training console can filter 'all ranks' vs 'rank 0 only'. Acceptance: the UI console has a rank filter dropdown; selecting rank 2 shows only that worker's lines, matching log.txt's per-rank prefixes.
**Reproducibility**
- [ ] **R-16** â€” Capture a full reproducibility manifest (config hash + env + seed + git SHA) per training run  `P1` `ph4` `backend`
    - The trainer seeds via helper.set_seed(args.train.seed, enable_full_determinism) and resumes RNG via torch_rng_state in extra_state, but nothing records the env (NNODES/NPROC_PER_NODE/CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES), torch/veomni versions, config_json hash, or engine git SHA alongside the run. Write a reproducibility.json into output_dir at start containing config_hash (sha256 of the resolved YAML), seed, parallel sizes, NCCL env, VeOmni submodule SHA, and torch build. Acceptance: GET /api/train/history/runs/{id}/reproducibility returns the manifest so a run is exactly re-runnable.
- [ ] **R-17** â€” Persist the resolved/expanded YAML not just the user-supplied one  `P2` `ph4` `backend`
    - veomni.utils.arguments.parse_args fills defaults and computes train_steps via compute_train_steps(max_seq_len, train_size, dataset_length) at runtime; the user-edited YAML (e.g. save_steps unset, log_steps=1) is not what actually ran. Snapshot args.train.to_dict()/args.data.to_dict() post-parse into output_dir/resolved_config.yaml so the recorded config reflects the effective hyperparams (train_steps, save_checkpoint_path, seed). Acceptance: the stored resolved_config differs from the input YAML exactly in the computed/derived keys.
**Resource guards**
- [ ] **R-18** â€” Add a live disk-space watchdog on the output_dir volume during training  `P1` `ph4` `backend`
    - The disk precheck is one-shot at start; a multi-epoch run fills output_dir continuously (dcp shards + hf_ckpt per save_steps) and can exhaust the volume mid-run, corrupting the in-flight Checkpointer.save. Add a background thread polling shutil.disk_usage(output_dir) every empty_cache_steps interval (the trainer already calls helper.empty_cache at L514/L536) and SIGTERM the torchrun group when free space drops below min(10GB, 1 shard) with status=failed reason='disk_full'. Acceptance: a run that would fill the disk aborts cleanly with the latest intact checkpoint preserved.
- [ ] **R-19** â€” Add a live VRAM OOM watchdog distinct from the static pre-flight estimator  `P2` `ph4` `backend`
    - The VRAM estimator is a static pre-flight (estimate_required_model_memory_gb); a real run can still OOM mid-step from fragmentation or a long-sequence batch (max_seq_len=2048 * 2 for block-diffusion concat). Add a watchdog reading torch.cuda.mem_get_info / nvidia-smi each empty_cache_steps; when free VRAM < 512MB on any rank, emit a 'vram_critical' SSE warning with per-GPU usage before the actual CUDA OOM, giving the UI a chance to suggest enabling enable_fsdp_offload or reducing micro_batch_size. Acceptance: the warning fires before torch's 'CUDA out of memory' terminates the process.

## 5. MoE Weight Processing: Merge / Split / Modeling File
<sub>28 items Â· section code `M`</sub>
**API - Merge**
- [ ] **M-1** â€” Implement POST /api/models/merge endpoint  `P0` `ph2` `backend`
    - Body takes {input_path, output_path} where input_path is the separate-expert HF dir. Service runs moe_merge over layers range(config.first_k_dense_replace, config.num_hidden_layers) for proj_types [gate_proj,up_proj,down_proj], stacking num_experts tensors with torch.stack(...,dim=0). Returns 202 with a job_id; rejects if any *.safetensors file is absent in input_path (the converter globs only *.safetensors).
**API - Split**
- [ ] **M-2** â€” Implement POST /api/models/split endpoint  `P0` `ph2` `backend`
    - Body takes {input_path, output_path, original_base_model_path}. input_path is the merged checkpoint. Service runs split_moe_experts, slicing each merged tensor[expert_id] for expert_id in range(config.num_experts). Returns 202 with job_id. Acceptance: each output tensor model.layers.{L}.mlp.experts.{id}.{proj}.weight has shape [4096,14336] for flash (moe_intermediate_size-derived), no expert dim.
**Config handling**
- [ ] **M-3** â€” Resolve num_experts/num_hidden_layers from config.json, never hardcode 8  `P0` `ph2` `backend`
    - Both configs/model_configs/llada2_mini/config.json and llada2_flash/config.json declare num_experts=256, contradicting the README's 8-expert example shapes [8,4096,14336]. Service must read these from the loaded AutoConfig and surface them in the API response. Add a guard that raises if config.num_experts is None (split_moe_experts already raises this ValueError).
- [ ] **M-4** â€” Handle trust_remote_code=True and auto_map wiring on output dirs  `P1` `ph2` `backend`
    - Input dir config.json carries auto_map (AutoConfig->configuration_llada2_moe.LLaDA2MoeConfig, AutoModelForCausalLM->modeling_llada2_moe.LLaDA2MoeModelLM, model_type llada2_moe_veomni). save_model_weights writes config+tokenizer assets; verify the emitted config.json retains model_type llada2_moe_veomni and architectures [LLaDA2MoeModelLM] so downstream trust_remote_code load works.
**Disk & resources**
- [ ] **M-5** â€” Precheck disk space before merge/split  `P0` `ph2` `infra`
    - Compute total bytes of *.safetensors in input_path (the converter loads all shards into RAM at once: state_dict = {} then iterates StateDictIterator). Require free space on output_path's volume >= input_size (merged output ~ same bytes; split ~ same bytes) AND warn if system RAM < input_size since the whole state dict is materialized in memory. Reject job with 507 if insufficient disk.
- [ ] **M-6** â€” Stream shards instead of full load for 100B flash to bound memory  `P1` `ph5` `backend`

Add an optional streaming path that merges/splits per-layer-per-proj as shards stream and writes incrementally, falling back to the in-memory path for mini. Gate behind a flag so default behavior matches the upstream script exactly.

The download endpoint must return the true resolved output path and the Hub/Inventory UI must show it; the Merge input picker must default to the resolved nested dir, not the bare local_dir the user typed, or merge silently finds no config.json and fails.

## 10. dLLM (Diffusion) Specific Capabilities & Eval
<sub>42 items Â· section code `DL`</sub>
**Attention Implementation**
- [ ] **DL-1** â€” Validate block-diffusion additive mask compatibility across attn_implementation eager/sdpa/flex_attention  `P0` `ph4` `backend`
    - Block-diffusion training builds a dense additive mask of shape [1,1,2*max_seq_len,2*max_seq_len] filled with -inf (train_llada2_bd.py L377-381) and passes it as attention_mask. The eager path does attn_weights + attention_mask (modeling L550) which works, but LLaDA2MoeSdpaAttention (L576) feeds the mask to scaled_dot_product_attention which rejects fully-masked rows and certain float dtypes under bf16. Add a test matrix: for block_diffusion_mode=true run one forward+backward with attn_implementation in {eager, sdpa, flex_attention} and assert no NaN/inf in loss; default the config editor to eager when block_diffusion_mode=true and warn that sdpa may be unstable.
**Chat Template**
- [ ] **DL-2** â€” Validate a diffusion-compatible chat template before training  `P0` `ph3` `backend`
    - train_llada2_bd.py raises 'No chat template found in the tokenizer' if tokenizer.chat_template is missing (L199-200); apply_chat_template_mdm (data_transform.py L124-140) tokenizes messages[:-1] as prompt and full messages as target. The dataset prep UI must assert the tokenizer attached to the merged model has a chat_template and show a preview of the prompt/response split used for masking.
- [ ] **DL-3** â€” Build prompt-only vs full-sequence tokenization for the mask boundary  `P1` `ph3` `backend`
    - process_mdm_sft_example (data_transform.py L26-41) computes prompt_length from messages[:-1] with add_generation_prompt=True, sets labels[:prompt_length]=-100, and only masks positions where maskable_mask = arange(max_seq_len) >= prompt_length. The dataset builder must emit this prompt_length boundary so non-prompt tokens are the only maskable ones.
**Checkpoint Export Gap**
- [ ] **DL-4** â€” Surface that only the final-epoch global_step gets an HF hf_ckpt export, not save_steps intermediates  `P1` `ph4` `backend`
    - train_llada2_bd.py only writes hf_ckpt for the value of save_checkpoint_path at loop end (L558-565), which is the last save_steps OR save_epochs checkpoint â€” earlier save_steps checkpoints remain DCP-only with no hf_ckpt subdir. The export/split pipeline (which requires hf_ckpt) therefore CANNOT target an intermediate step. The checkpoints scanner must flag global_step dirs lacking hf_ckpt as 'not exportable' and the UI must disable Split on them, with a tooltip explaining only the final step is HF-serialized.
**Checkpoint Format**
- [ ] **DL-5** â€” Ensure diffusion SFT checkpoints save with block_diffusion_mode metadata  `P1` `ph5` `backend`
    - save_args persists the full args dataclass (train_llada2_bd.py L180) and save_model_assets stores model_config+tokenizer (L321). The export step must record block_diffusion_mode, block_size, same_token_labels, and noise_range in the checkpoint's args.json so a downstream split+inference run can validate the checkpoint was trained in diffusion mode.
**Confidence Loss**
- [ ] **DL-6** â€” Expose confidence_beta auxiliary loss from the dparallel trainer  `P2` `ph4` `fullstack`
    - train_llada2_bd_with_dparallel.py adds train.confidence_beta (default 0.0, L102-105) and compute_confidence_loss() (L173-203): the mean entropy of the output distribution at correctly-predicted positions, added as consistency_loss + confidence_loss*confidence_beta. The dparallel config form must expose confidence_beta with help text 'weight for confidence loss entropy of correct predictions; 0 disables'.
**Continuous SFT**
- [ ] **DL-7** â€” Implement continuous SFT resume across noise schedules  `P1` `ph4` `backend`
    - README Features list '(Continuous) supervised-finetuning' and train_llada2_bd.py supports load_checkpoint_path (L348-362) restoring global_step, lr_scheduler, train_dataloader, environ_meter and torch RNG. The training service must support launching a second SFT run pointed at a previous hf_ckpt/dcp checkpoint without re-merging, preserving the diffusion RNG state used for noise sampling.
**Dataset Prep - Masking Integrity**
- [ ] **DL-8** â€” Guard against mask_token_id collisions in raw assistant text during dataset build  `P1` `ph3` `backend`
    - process_mdm_sft_example computes loss_mask = (noisy_input_ids == mask_token_id) (data_transform.py L40), then sets labels[~loss_mask] = -100. If the ORIGINAL assistant tokens already contain id 156895, those positions are mis-counted as 'masked' even when not flipped by sft_noise_transition, silently widening the supervised set. The dataset builder must scan each built JSONL's tokenized assistant spans for occurrences of 156895 and reject/warn when count>0, since a diffusion mask token should never appear in ground-truth text.
**Diffusion Playground**
- [ ] **DL-9** â€” Build a step-by-step unmasking visualization in the diffusion playground  `P1` `ph8` `frontend`
    - generate() iterates denoising_steps_per_block over blocks and per-step replaces mask_id tokens with sampled x0 only where confidence > threshold (L1491-1524). The playground UI must render the per-step token grid (mask vs revealed vs high-confidence) by capturing x0, x0_p, and transfer_index at each step from a streaming/generate-trace endpoint, not just final text.
- [ ] **DL-10** â€” Add a generation-quality-vs-steps sweep control to the playground  `P2` `ph8` `frontend`
    - Because steps and block_length trade quality for speed (generate docstring L1410-1414), the playground must let the user run the same prompt across a steps array (e.g. 8/16/32/64) and block_length array and diff the decoded outputs side by side, surfacing the quality/latency frontier specific to diffusion decoding.
**Eval - Diffusion Calibration**
- [ ] **DL-11** â€” Add mask-ratio / noise-schedule calibration eval reporting loss stratified by sampled sigma  `P2` `ph10` `backend`
    - sft_noise_transition samples sigma uniformly in [noise_range_low, noise_range_high] per example (data_transform.py L116) but training collapses all sigmas into one loss scalar. Add an eval pass that buckets held-out loss by sigma quartile and reports a sigma-vs-loss curve, so users can see whether the model is weak at high-noise (early denoising) or low-noise (late refinement) regimes â€” a diffusion-specific diagnostic with no autoregressive equivalent. Expose via GET /api/train/runs/{id}/noise-calibration.
**Eval - Masked-LM Pitfall**
- [ ] **DL-12** â€” Prevent autoregressive eval harnesses (perplexity, LM-Evaluation-Harness) from running unmasked on a dLLM  `P1` `ph10` `backend`
    - Standard perplexity and most LM-Evaluation-Harness tasks assume causal next-token scoring, but LLaDA2 is a masked-diffusion model whose forward returns same-position logits meant to predict masked tokens, not next tokens (same_token_labels=true path, train_llada2_bd.py L452-458). The eval/integration layer must BLOCK plugging the dLLM into AR perplexity/gsm8k log-likelihood harnesses directly (would produce nonsense scores) and only permit the diffusion-native eval (mask-prediction accuracy, steps-sweep) â€” surface this as a hard guard with a cited rationale.
**Eval Metrics**
- [ ] **DL-13** â€” Implement mask-prediction accuracy as a diffusion eval metric  `P1` `ph10` `backend`
    - Training computes per-token CE on noisy->clean positions (labels==-100 elsewhere, data_transform.py L40-41). Add an eval job that, given a held-out set, reports mask-prediction top-1 accuracy at the masked positions (argmax(noisy_logits) == labels where labels!=-100), a metric with no autoregressive equivalent.
- [ ] **DL-14** â€” Report throughput in tokens/step and tokens/sec for diffusion decoding  `P1` `ph10` `backend`
    - generate() produces block_length tokens per denoising iteration but re-runs the full forward each step (L1496). The eval harness must report effective tokens/sec accounting for the recompute, plus tokens-unmasked-per-forward, so users see the parallel-decoding speedup vs autoregressive baselines.
- [ ] **DL-15** â€” Add generation quality at varying denoising steps to the eval report  `P1` `ph10` `backend`
    - Since steps (L1393) and threshold (L1399) materially change output quality, the eval report must run the test split at multiple steps values and record a quality metric (e.g. GSM8K exact-match for the gsm8k_datasets test.jsonl) per steps setting, producing a steps-vs-quality curve rather than a single perplexity number.
- [ ] **DL-16** â€” Replace perplexity-only reporting with diffusion-aware loss decomposition  `P2` `ph8` `fullstack`
    - The dparallel trainer logs training/cons_loss and training/conf_loss separately (L548, L554). The training dashboard must plot consistency loss and confidence loss as distinct series (not a single 'loss'), and avoid presenting autoregressive perplexity as the headline metric for a diffusion model.
**Inference - Greedy Determinism**
- [ ] **DL-17** â€” Document and gate temperature=0.0 greedy diffusion decoding vs sampling determinism  `P2` `ph8` `frontend`
    - generate() defaults temperature=0.0 (L1391) meaning greedy argmax over active_logits at each denoising step (via _sample_with_temperature_topk_topp), which for diffusion is deterministic per step but still depends on block ordering. The inference settings UI must label temperature=0 as 'greedy (deterministic per block)' and warn that unlike autoregressive greedy, output can still vary if block_length/steps change, since the denoising order differs. Expose a 'deterministic' badge only when temperature=0 AND fixed block_length/steps/seed.
**Inference - Prompt/Block Alignment**
- [ ] **DL-18** â€” Fix prompt_length not divisible by block_length causing partial-block prompt corruption at inference  `P0` `ph6` `backend`
    - generate() computes prefill_blocks = prompt_length // block_length with integer division (L1477) then starts denoising at block index prefill_blocks. If prompt_length % block_length != 0, the prompt's trailing partial block is treated as a generation block: its tail tokens get overwritten by sampled x0 (L1524), corrupting the prompt. The inference layer must right-pad the prompt to a multiple of block_length (or left-pad and recompute position_ids) before calling generate(), and surface the padded length so the UI can warn when a chat prompt lands mid-block.
**Inference - Semi-Autoregressive**
- [ ] **DL-19** â€” Expose the block-wise semi-autoregressive decoding boundary in the chat stream metadata  `P1` `ph6` `fullstack`
    - generate() processes blocks sequentially (for num_block in range(prefill_blocks, num_blocks), L1483) and only the active block's last block_length tokens are denoised per step, so tokens commit block-by-block rather than globally. The SSE diffusion_frame events must include {block_index, num_blocks, block_length} so the UI can render the block frontier advancing across the canvas, distinct from a global denoise. This block-boundary metadata is what makes the decoding 'semi-autoregressive' and must not be flattened into a single step counter.
**Inference Params**
- [ ] **DL-20** â€” Expose block_length, steps, and gen_length as first-class inference controls  `P0` `ph6` `fullstack`
    - model.generate() (modeling_llada2_moe.py L1388-1402) takes block_length=32, steps=32, gen_length=2048. The inference/chat request schema must include these three plus temperature, top_k, top_p. steps is capped at steps=min(steps, gen_length // minimal_topk) (L1452); the UI must warn when requested steps exceed this cap.
- [ ] **DL-21** â€” Expose threshold (confidence acceptance) and eos_early_stop as inference params  `P1` `ph6` `fullstack`
    - generate() uses threshold=0.95 (L1399) to accept sampled tokens only when prob > threshold, falling back to topk by confidence (L1510-1521), and eos_early_stop=False (L1397) with eos_id=156892. The chat/inference UI must expose threshold as 'Token confidence threshold' and eos_early_stop as a checkbox, since they directly affect generation quality and stop behavior.
- [ ] **DL-22** â€” Expose minimal_topk step-capping parameter in advanced inference settings  `P2` `ph6` `frontend`
    - generate() computes effective steps = min(steps, gen_length // minimal_topk) (L1452, default minimal_topk=1). Expose minimal_topk under an 'Advanced' inference section so power users can reduce the step cap for faster (lower-quality) decoding, with live recalculation of the effective step count shown next to the steps field.
**Inference Params - Autoregressive Assumptions**

    - modeling_llada2_moe.py LLaDA2MoeModelLM.generate() (L1388-1402) accepts temperature, block_length, steps, gen_length, top_p, top_k, eos_early_stop, minimal_topk, threshold, eos_id, mask_id ONLY â€” grep confirms zero occurrences of 'repetition_penalty' in the file. The existing inference-schema item lists repetition_penalty as a dLLM param; that is an autoregressive assumption that would silently no-op. Remove it from DiffusionGenerationParams and from the chat settings UI; document that repetition penalties are not applied during block-diffusion unmasking.

The VRAM/throughput estimator must model recompute cost as O(steps * window_tokens) and warn that diffusion inference does not benefit from a KV cache.

The dataset prep and inference docs must document 156895 as the LLaDA2 mask token and refuse configs that change it without a matching tokenizer change.

Add a verification step that merge -> train one step -> split -> merge yields byte-identical expert tensor shapes and that num_experts=256 (flash) / config num_experts is preserved across the export pipeline (Phase 5).

Because moe_merge/split only touch gate/up/down_proj weights and copy the router (gate) verbatim, add an integration assertion that after a round-trip the router logits distribution on a fixed input is bit-identical pre-merge vs post-split, confirming the sigmoid-grouped top-k routing is not disturbed by expert stacking.

Add an optional eval/diagnostic that, on a held-out batch, reads topk_idx from LLaDA2MoeMoE.forward (modeling L365) and reports per-expert assignment frequency + entropy; warn in the training dashboard if any expert receives <1% of tokens.

The config form must disable expert_parallel_size>0 when moe_implementation=eager and default to fused (YAML moe_implementation: fused) for both mini and flash.

- [ ] **DL-33** â€” Block pad-token/rmpad misalignment guard for the diffusion additive mask  `P1` `ph4` `backend`
    - data_transform.py pads to max_length (L136) and the block mask is built for exactly max_seq_len*2 positions keyed off absolute index n=max_seq_len (train_llada2_bd.py L374). If train.rmpad or rmpad_with_pos_ids (YAML L28-29) removes padding, the noisy/clean halves are no longer max_seq_len wide and the index-based block mask silently misaligns with real tokens. Add a config validator: block_diffusion_mode=true REQUIRES rmpad=false and rmpad_with_pos_ids=false (or fixed-length padding), failing at /api/train/config write with the rationale.
**Reproducibility**
- [ ] **DL-34** â€” Pin and document the per-worker RNG state for sft_noise_transition reproducibility across resume  `P2` `ph4` `backend`
    - sft_noise_transition calls torch.rand(1) and torch.rand(*x_0.shape) per example (data_transform.py L116,L120) INSIDE DataLoader workers, but resume only restores the main-process torch_rng_state (train_llada2_bd.py L357) and the dataloader state_dict â€” not each worker's RNG seed. On resume, the sampled noise ratios sigma and move_indices diverge from a continuous run, so resumed training is not bit-reproducible. Document this limit, and for enable_full_determinism=true inject a per-example seed derived from (global_step, example_index) so noise is reproducible regardless of worker scheduling.
**Safety - Remote Code**
- [ ] **DL-35** â€” Pin and verify the modeling_llada2_moe.py fingerprint matches the trained checkpoint architecture before inference load  `P1` `ph6` `backend`
    - README step 2 copies modeling_llada2_moe.py from the ORIGINAL pre-merge base dir into the split dir, but if the engine or VeOmni is upgraded between train and inference, the copied modeling file may diverge from the architecture the checkpoint was trained against (e.g. block_diffusion_mask semantics, generate() signature). On /api/inference/load, hash modeling_llada2_moe.py and compare against the hash recorded at train time in args.json/save_args; refuse load with a 'modeling file drift' error on mismatch, offering to re-copy from the pinned engine version.
**Token-ID Provenance**
- [ ] **DL-36** â€” Reconcile eos_id/pad_token_id collision (156892) and config-vs-code pad_token_id mismatch (156892 vs 126081)  `P0` `ph6` `backend`
    - config.json sets pad_token_id=156892 (mini L52, flash L52) while configuration_llada2_moe.py defaults pad_token_id=126081 (L35); generate() hardcodes eos_id=156892 (L1400) which COLLIDES with the config pad token. Add a load-time assertion that resolves pad/eos/mask ids from the tokenizer and fails loudly if eos_id==pad_token_id or if config.pad_token_id != the Python default is not reconciled. Surface the resolved {pad_id, eos_id, mask_id} triple in /api/inference/status so a stale config.json cannot silently break eos_early_stop.
- [ ] **DL-37** â€” Validate mask_token_id (156895) exists in the tokenizer vocabulary before training and inference  `P1` `ph3` `backend`
    - mask_token_id=156895 is hardcoded in train_llada2_bd.py (L208,L216), data_transform.py (L37,L86), and generate() (L1401) but is ABSENT from config.json (no mask_token_id key). If a non-LLaDA2 tokenizer or a re-tokenized dataset is used, 156895 may be out-of-vocab or map to an unrelated token. Add a preflight check: tokenizer.convert_ids_to_tokens(156895) must succeed and, ideally, equal the tokenizer's mask special token; fail training/inference load with a precise error otherwise.
**Trainable Parallel Decoding**
- [ ] **DL-38** â€” Flag trainable parallel decoding as a roadmap item in the UI  `P2` `ph8` `frontend`
    - README TODO marks 'Trainable Parallel Decoding' as in-progress (L38). The features panel must render this as a disabled/roadmap badge (not a clickable toggle) and link to the dFactory roadmap, so users do not expect current block_length/steps to be learned during SFT.
**Training Loss**
- [ ] **DL-39** â€” Expose block-diffusion vs full-attention training mode as a first-class toggle  `P0` `ph4` `fullstack`
    - Mirror train.block_diffusion_mode (train_llada2_bd.py L101-104: True = block_diffusion, False = full_attention). The training-config form must render a boolean that, when true, drives the noisy/clean input concat (full_input_ids = cat[noisy, clean]) and the custom block-diagonal attention mask build; when false, attention_mask is passed as None.
- [ ] **DL-40** â€” Expose block_size as a validated training parameter  `P0` `ph4` `fullstack`
    - train.block_size (default 32, train_llada2_bd.py L105-108) defines the diffusion block granularity and must be a positive int the UI surfaces; it feeds both the block_diffusion_mask() call and bd_attn_full_len = max_seq_len * 2 mask construction (L366-375). Validate block_size <= max_seq_len and that max_seq_len % 1 is consistent with the mask build.
- [ ] **DL-41** â€” Expose same_token_labels to switch loss head between same-token and shifted  `P1` `ph4` `fullstack`
    - train.same_token_labels (train_llada2_bd.py L109-112) toggles between same-position mask-prediction CE loss (no shift, reduction summed / (labels!=-100)) and next-token-shifted CE. The UI must label these as 'Mask-prediction (same token)' vs 'Next-token shifted' so users understand it is not standard LM loss.
**VRAM - Diffusion 2x Sequence**
- [ ] **DL-42** â€” Model the 2x-sequence-length activation cost of full_input_ids=cat[noisy,clean] in the VRAM estimator  `P1` `ph4` `backend`
    - In block_diffusion_mode the trainer concatenates noisy+clean into full_input_ids of length 2*max_seq_len (L428) and runs the full model over both halves with the dense block mask, so activation memory is ~2x a same-length autoregressive forward (plus the [1,1,2L,2L] mask materialized on-GPU). The existing BD-SFT VRAM estimator must add an explicit 'diffusion_2x_activation' term scaling activations by 2x and the mask by (2*max_seq_len)^2 elements; warn that this dominates at max_seq_len=2048 even for mini.

## 14. Design System & Visual Direction (distinct, non-slop)
<sub>26 items Â· section code `DS`</sub>
**Alternative proposals**
- [ ] **DS-1** â€” Proposal A: 'Oscilloscope' â€” phosphor-green-on-dark instrument direction  `P2` `ph7` `design`
    - Direction option for the user to pick. Dark-first: near-black `#0e0f0d` panel, CRT phosphor green `#33ff66` as the single accent for live training curves, faint scanline texture, mono-forward. Distinct from Unsloth (warm light + green accent) and from black+blue AI-slop. Best for the live-training console.
- [ ] **DS-2** â€” Proposal B: 'Blueprint' â€” cyan-on-vellum technical-drawing direction  `P2` `ph7` `design`
    - Direction option for the user to pick. Light-first: warm vellum `#f4efe4` paper, deep navy ink `#13243a`, and a single cyan `#0ea5b7` accent with a 1px hairline grid background. Reads like an engineering schematic for the pipeline DAG. Distinct from both Unsloth and AI-slop; good for the model-hub/dataset-prep surfaces.
- [ ] **DS-3** â€” Proposal C: 'Benchtop' â€” warm-neutral lab-instrument direction with amber accent  `P2` `ph7` `design`
    - Direction option for the user to pick (and the current default). Warm bone-white surfaces, ink text, amber `#d97706` single sharp accent, brushed-metal neutrals, dense numeric readouts. Evokes a physical lab instrument rather than a chat app or a SaaS dashboard. This is the direction the P0 token/typography items assume; picking A or B requires re-tokening those items.

Wire TrainingLossChartCard / GradNormChartCard / LearningRateChartCard / EvalLossChartCard equivalents.

**Design-language declaration**
- [ ] **DS-11** â€” Name the dFactory-Lab design language and document its anti-pattern charter  `P0` `ph7` `design`
    - Write a DESIGN.md that names a deliberate language (e.g. 'Benchtop') and explicitly forbids the AI-slop set: no black+blue gradients, no ShimmerButton conic-gradient (the `rgba(0,0,0,1)` + `--shimmer-color` pattern in studio's shimmer-button.tsx), no SparklesText purple/pink (`#9E7AFF`/`#FE8BBB`), no ShineBorder animated-gradient borders. Contrast against Unsloth's green-primary warm-off-white identity, not imitate it.
**Iconography**
- [ ] **DS-12** â€” Standardize on a single icon family and an --icon-size token  `P1` `ph7` `design`
    - Unsloth mixes `@hugeicons/react` + `@hugeicons/core-free-icons` + `lucide-react` and pins `--icon-size:18px` + `.size-icon` (index.css). Pick ONE family for dFactory-Lab (e.g. lucide-react only, or Phosphor) and a single `--icon-size` token; forbid the mixed two-family approach so the iconography reads as one coherent set tied to pipeline verbs (download, merge, split, train, convert, chat).
**Motion**
- [ ] **DS-13** â€” Author motion principles and an easing curve set distinct from Unsloth's Emil-Kowalski curves  `P1` `ph7` `design`
    - Unsloth declares `--ease-out-quart`/`--ease-out-cubic` and `--duration-micro/fast/normal` (100/150/200ms). Define dFactory-Lab's own timing tokens with a slightly snappier feel (e.g. `--ease-lab: cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1)`, 90/140/220ms) and a written principle: motion communicates pipeline progress (block-diffusion steps advancing), never decorates. Wire tw-animate-css but override the easing tokens.
- [ ] **DS-14** â€” Use tw-shimmer only for determinate/indeterminate pipeline progress, never as decoration  `P1` `ph7` `design`
    - Unsloth uses shimmer widely (artifact-card-shimmer, loading-bar-slide). Restrict dFactory-Lab shimmer to progress: a 1/3-width sweeping segment for indeterminate MoE-merge / download steps (adapt Unsloth's `.loading-bar-slide` 1.3s keyframe) and a determinate fill for torchrun training progress. Forbid the decorative ShimmerButton conic-gradient entirely.
- [ ] **DS-15** â€” Honor prefers-reduced-motion across the app including pipeline animations  `P1` `ph7` `design`
    - Replicate Unsloth's robust reduced-motion block (index.css `@media (prefers-reduced-motion:reduce)` collapsing transitions to 0.01ms) but extend it to dFactory-Lab's diffusion-step animations and chart redraws. Keep only determinate progress bars and spinners animating, exactly as Unsloth whitelists `.animate-spin` and `.loading-bar-slide`.
**Platform contrast modes**
- [ ] **DS-16** â€” Add Windows forced-colors (high-contrast) and prefers-contrast: more support  `P2` `ph9` `design`
    - Windows is a primary platform; add @media (forced-colors: active) rules exposing the stage status and pipeline DAG via system colors and visible focus outlines, plus @media (prefers-contrast: more) that boosts border contrast and disables the hairline-only elevation. Verify the export stepper and GPU telemetry remain legible under Windows High Contrast mode.
**Spacing / elevation / radius**
- [ ] **DS-17** â€” Establish a tighter radius scale than Unsloth's rounded chat aesthetic  `P1` `ph7` `design`
    - Unsloth pins `--radius:1.1rem` (very round, chat-app feel) and pill `rounded-full` buttons (button.tsx). For an instrument/lab console, set a smaller base radius (e.g. `--radius:0.5rem`) and define a scale: `--radius-xs..2xl`. Default Buttons to rounded-md (not full pills) to read as a lab tool, reserving pills only for status chips.
- [ ] **DS-18** â€” Define an elevation system that rejects Gemini-style soft shadows in favor of hairline borders  `P1` `ph7` `design`
    - Unsloth leans on soft drop shadows (`box-shadow:0 2px 8px -2px rgba(0,0,0,0.16)` on composer/menu/dialog, dropped in dark). For lab-grade clarity, prefer 1px hairline borders from `--border` over shadows, and define only 2 elevation levels (rest: border only; elevated: border + 1 subtle shadow). Document that dark mode keeps borders, unlike Unsloth which drops shadow to none.
- [ ] **DS-19** â€” Define a 4px-based spacing rhythm and dense console density rules  `P2` `ph7` `design`
    - Unsloth uses `--spacing:0.25rem` (4px) but loose chat padding. Define a lab-console density spec: base 4px grid, compact control heights (h-7 inputs vs Unsloth h-9), tighter table row padding, and a 13px base UI font. Codify a `density:comfortable|compact` token switch so the dense training-console surfaces differ from the roomy chat surface.
**States**
- [ ] **DS-20** â€” Author empty-state system using the Empty primitive pattern with dFactory-Lab iconography  `P1` `ph8` `frontend`
    - Unsloth's empty.tsx defines Empty/EmptyMedia/EmptyTitle/EmptyDescription with a dashed border + `text-muted-foreground`. Build dFactory-Lab equivalents scoped to pipeline stages (no models downloaded, no datasets built, no training runs, no checkpoints to split) each with a stage-tinted EmptyMedia icon and a primary CTA â€” avoiding the generic 'nothing here' look.
- [ ] **DS-21** â€” Author loading-state system: skeleton + indeterminate bar + diffusion-step animation  `P1` `ph8` `frontend`
    - Combine three loaders keyed to operation length: skeleton (dataset preview rows, model card grid â€” adapt Unsloth's skeleton.tsx), indeterminate sweeping bar (MoE merge, HF download â€” `.loading-bar-slide`), and a diffusion-step 'denoising' animation for inference/chat that visually echoes block-diffusion. All must use the metric-mono style and stay calm (no sparkles/shimmer decoration).
- [ ] **DS-22** â€” Author error-state system with copyable error chips for CLI stderr  `P1` `ph8` `frontend`
    - Adapt Unsloth's copyable-error-chip.tsx pattern but style for a lab console: monospace stderr block, a red `--destructive` left rule (not a full card), stage-tinted header (e.g. 'torchrun failed at step N'), and a copy button. Distinct from Unsloth's soft chat toast styling since dFactory-Lab surfaces raw pipeline failures.
**Theme parity / accessibility**
- [ ] **DS-23** â€” Guarantee light + dark theme parity with shared token names  `P1` `ph7` `design`
    - Unsloth maintains separate hex values per theme (light `--background:#fefefd`, dark `#1a1b1e`) under `:root`/`.dark`. Define dFactory-Lab tokens with identical NAMES in both themes and a parity rule: every token present in `:root` must exist in `.dark`, radius must match across themes (Unsloth explicitly keeps `--radius:1.1rem` in dark). Add a CI check that diffs the token sets.
- [ ] **DS-24** â€” Verify WCAG AA contrast for every token pair, especially the sharp accent on surfaces  `P1` `ph7` `design`
    - The single sharp accent (amber/vermilion) is the highest-risk pair. Define and test every text-on-surface combination (foreground on background, primary on primary-foreground, muted-foreground on background, stage colors on card) to >=4.5:1 (normal text) / >=3:1 (large + UI components). Unsloth's `--muted-foreground: oklch(0.5486 0 0)` on warm white is borderline; dFactory-Lab must clear AA explicitly.
**Typography**
- [ ] **DS-25** â€” Pick a distinct display+body+mono type pairing and wire fontsource imports  `P0` `ph7` `design`
    - Unsloth uses Hellix/Inter/Fira Code (index.css lines 9-11 + @fontsource-variable/figtree,inter,space-grotesk in package.json). Pick a DIFFERENT pairing to stay visually distinct: e.g. Space Grotesk Variable (display, technical), Inter Tight (body), and JetBrains Mono / IBM Plex Mono for code/metrics. Import via @fontsource-variable and set `--font-display`, `--font-sans`, `--font-mono` in `@theme inline`.
- [ ] **DS-26** â€” Define a numeric/type-metrics style for step counts, loss values, token counters  `P1` `ph7` `design`
    - Add `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` and a `text-metric` utility class for the dense numeric readouts in the training console (step N/max, loss, grad-norm, learning rate, diffusion steps). Unsloth uses `font-mono font-medium tabular-nums` inline (chart.tsx ChartTooltipContent value span); centralize it as one token-backed class so every metric column aligns.

### Notes & provenance

- **285** domain items + **54** critique items were authored by a multi-agent workflow that read the real dFactory and
  Unsloth Studio source (exact files, params, line-level behavior). Three domains that hit transient API rate limits
  (**Model Hub**, **Datasets**, **Auth/Security/Observability**) were authored by hand from the same source files.
  De-duplication is by normalized title; complementary items were retained.

  `python scripts/build_checklist.py`.
